description_long: This is a single-story log cabin constructed primarily from timber logs, featuring a broad, dark gabled hip roof with deep overhangs. The exterior walls are composed of horizontally stacked logs, giving the structure a rustic and robust appearance. Large, multi-pane windows are symmetrically arranged on the main facades, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The cabin sits on a stone or concrete foundation, elevating it slightly above ground level. A small, covered porch with steps is present at the main entrance, providing a sheltered transition into the building. The overall design is compact yet spacious, suitable for residential use in both urban and natural settings.
description: A timber log cabin with a wide, dark hip roof, large windows, and a small covered porch, set on a stone foundation. The structure exudes a rustic, residential charm.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main facade with two large windows, the covered porch, and the deep overhang of the hip roof.
description_view_1: The front view shows the symmetrical arrangement of three large windows and the prominent gable end of the roof.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the side extension of the cabin, additional windows, and the continuation of the roof overhang.
description_view_3: The rear view displays a long, uninterrupted wall with a set of windows and the full span of the hip roof.
attribute_roof_type: hip roof with deep overhangs, constructed from dark roofing material, no visible dormers
attribute_window_arrangement: Symmetrical arrangement of large, rectangular multi-pane windows on the main facades; three grouped on the front, two on the side, and several spaced along the rear wall
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door located at the front facade, accessed via a small covered porch
